rules.name: Word spawning CMD
rules.explanation: Microsoft Word launched a command shell. This is a classic malware macro execution pattern.
rules.recommendation: Immediately terminate this process. Check for macro-enabled documents.

rules.name: Word spawning PowerShell
rules.explanation: Microsoft Word launched PowerShell. Macros may be executing encoded commands.
rules.recommendation: Terminate the PowerShell process and disable macros in Word.

rules.name: Excel spawning CMD
rules.explanation: Microsoft Excel launched a command shell. Possible macro-based attack.
rules.recommendation: Terminate the process and review Excel macros.

rules.name: Unsigned temp executable
rules.explanation: An unsigned executable is running from a temporary folder. This is a common malware staging pattern.
rules.recommendation: Terminate the process and scan with antivirus.

rules.name: Unsigned with suspicious parent
rules.explanation: An unsigned process was spawned by an unexpected parent. This indicates possible process injection.
rules.recommendation: Investigate the parent-child relationship in Task Manager.

rules.name: Suspicious port connection
rules.explanation: Connection to a port commonly used by attack frameworks (Metasploit, Cobalt Strike, etc.).
rules.recommendation: Block this process in Windows Firewall immediately.

rules.name: Foreign IP connection
rules.explanation: A process is connecting to an IP address outside your local network.
rules.recommendation: Verify the connection is expected. Block if unauthorized.

rules.name: Camera background access
rules.explanation: The camera was activated while the application was running in the background.
rules.recommendation: If you did not start a video call, terminate the process.

rules.name: Unknown camera access
rules.explanation: This application has never accessed your camera before.
rules.recommendation: Verify this is a legitimate application you recently installed.

rules.name: Ransomware file burst
rules.explanation: Over 10 files were modified in 3 seconds with ransomware-associated extensions. This is a strong indicator of active encryption.
rules.recommendation: Disconnect from network immediately. Do not restart. Isolate the machine.

rules.name: Ransomware extension detected
rules.explanation: A file with a known ransomware extension was detected. Ransomware may be encrypting your files.
rules.recommendation: Disconnect from network. Do not pay the ransom. Contact IT support.

rules.name: System directory modification
rules.explanation: Files in a Windows system directory were modified. This may indicate tampering.
rules.recommendation: Run System File Checker (sfc /scannow) and review recent changes.

rules.name: Anomalous idle activity
rules.explanation: CPU usage during idle is 2x higher than baseline. Unknown processes may be active.
rules.recommendation: Check Task Manager for unexpected high-CPU processes.

rules.name: Unknown process during idle
rules.explanation: Multiple unknown processes are active while the system is idle.
rules.recommendation: Review running processes and terminate anything suspicious.
